finding_results.notes: The post-couch section now runs 59 lines from the confession rehearsal through the final image, cuts the abstract moral-summary loops, preserves the rehearsal material, and closes on Nate staying in bed while Sarah shifts on the couch.
finding_results.summary: The chapter tail is compressed, concrete, and ends on action rather than thesis.

finding_results.notes: Sarah's triple sentence now uses contractions throughout while preserving the year-of-life, kitchen, and name-as-prank structure; the chapter also no longer uses uncontracted 'do not' or 'not because... but because' constructions.
finding_results.summary: Sarah's pressure-register now sounds spoken rather than composed.

finding_results.notes: Roughened all three of Sarah's most workshop-finished lines. (1) Line 237: Replaced the 'passport' extended metaphor ('That's not a passport. You don't get to stamp that on the counter and walk past the rest.') with a messier spoken version that uses a false start, self-interruption, and slight repetition ('You can't just say that and — what, I'm supposed to nod and stop asking questions? That doesn't work. You know that doesn't work.'). (2) Line 375: Replaced the 'somebody else's coat' literary metaphor with a concrete domestic observation ('This is not stress. You've been walking around this apartment for months like you're still figuring out where the light switches are.'), breaking the tricolon pattern and using 'This is not stress' as a formal-under-pressure self-correction consistent with Sarah's stress tell of getting more precise. (3) Line 449: Replaced the compressed aphorism ('That sentence only works if there's an alternative you're offering me.') with Sarah quoting Nate back and asking practical questions ("'Please don't do this.' OK. What should I do instead? Just go back to the bed because you said please?"), making the refusal concrete and question-driven rather than epigrammatic. All three revisions preserve Sarah's argumentative leverage (rejection of deflection, rejection of the stress cover story, demand for an alternative) while adding friction through false starts, quoting-back, repetition, and concrete domestic reference. None of the revised lines scan as standalone maxims.

pass_summaries.summary: Revised the cited clipped narration, tuned Sarah's confrontation register toward high-contraction pressured speech, and rebuilt the post-couch aftermath into one dominant near-confession that ends on Nate's concrete non-confession.

Single finding addressed. Roughened three of Sarah's cleanest quotable lines — the 'passport' rebuke (line 237), the 'somebody else's coat' metaphor (line 375), and the compressed 'sentence only works if' alternative-offer line (line 449) — replacing each with messier spoken phrasing that uses false starts, self-interruption, quoting-back, repetition, and concrete domestic reference. All three retain their original argumentative leverage and Sarah's teacherly precision under pressure, but none would scan as standalone aphorisms. No other chapter text was modified; heading contract, continuity, and character voice signatures preserved throughout.
Varied the confrontation and aftermath landing beats to remove the chapter-level negation/correction formula while preserving scene pressure, Sarah's precision, and Nate's guilt-ridden interiority.
